artifact.content_preview:
From: dave.chen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration
Date: March 7, 2026

Alice, Carol,

While reviewing the network migration plan for Site A, I wanted to flag
an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

Given that our contractors will already be on-site and the network
infrastructure will be partially disrupted during the Catalyst 9500
switch deployme

extracted_tasks.text: Discuss Wi-Fi infrastructure upgrade opportunity with the steering committee
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I wanted to flag an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points from Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Upgrading to the Catalyst 9120 series during the same migration window would avoid a separate disruptive maintenance activity later in the year

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain steering committee approval for the expanded scope and budget
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Could we add this to the scope and get steering committee approval before the March 18 window?

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Leverage existing on-site contractors and network disruption for Wi-Fi upgrade
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: this would be an ideal time to also upgrade the Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities.

extracted_tasks.text: Procure Catalyst 9120 access points via TechSupplier
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I've already spoken informally with TechSupplier and they can include the access points in the same delivery.

metadata.subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration

{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026
D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026
D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval
All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor
Compliance required with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach
Compliance required with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
Compliance required with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
Compliance required with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
Compliance required with Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.15,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites; Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones. OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative (by analogy, network hardware not listed in scope baseline). CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes — expenditures beyond approved budget require formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure; All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor.",
  "suggested_action": "Do not proceed informally. Dave Chen must submit a formal Change Request through the SSC Project Change Management Process before any commitments are made to TechSupplier or the steering committee. The Change Request must document: (1) full scope delta — Wi-Fi AP replacement is not listed in the baseline and constitutes a new deliverable; (2) budget impact of $85,000–$110,000 against the approved $2,400,000 CAD ceiling, noting that even the low estimate (~3.5% of total budget) requires Director General of IT Infrastructure approval, and the PM should assess whether cumulative variances risk breaching the 15% Treasury Board threshold; (3) schedule impact of 9 additional days against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint for D-006 — with Go-Live only ~54 days away from the email date, this risk must be formally assessed; (4) written approval from the Project Sponsor prior to any vendor engagement or procurement action. The steering committee discussion Dave references is appropriate as an escalation venue, but only after a formal Change Request is drafted, not as a substitute for it. Additionally, note that Sites B and C are slated for decommissioning — investing in Wi-Fi infrastructure at those sites requires explicit justification and may be inconsistent with the decommissioning mandate.",
  "reasoning": "The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clear scope expansion. The approved scope baseline explicitly limits network hardware work to Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switch deployment and associated network segmentation and BGP migration activities. Wireless access point replacement is a distinct hardware category not referenced anywhere in the in-scope items. The out-of-scope exclusion of network monitoring tool upgrades — deferred to a separate initiative — establishes a pattern that infrastructure upgrades beyond the defined baseline are intentionally excluded, and the same logic applies here. The cost estimate of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on an already fully committed $2,400,000 CAD envelope, triggering mandatory Director General approval. The 9-day timeline addition is a material schedule risk given the hard April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006) and the ADM-level approval required for any extension beyond June 30, 2026. Furthermore, Dave's informal engagement with TechSupplier prior to any formal change approval is a procurement governance concern that the PM should address directly. The efficiency argument (contractors already on-site, reduced future disruption) has operational merit and may justify pursuing the change — but only through proper channels, not as an informal add-on."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites; Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones. OUT OF SCOPE: Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative (by analogy, network hardware not listed in scope baseline). CONSTRAINTS: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes — expenditures beyond approved budget require formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure; All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor.
parsed_json.suggested_action: Do not proceed informally. Dave Chen must submit a formal Change Request through the SSC Project Change Management Process before any commitments are made to TechSupplier or the steering committee. The Change Request must document: (1) full scope delta — Wi-Fi AP replacement is not listed in the baseline and constitutes a new deliverable; (2) budget impact of $85,000–$110,000 against the approved $2,400,000 CAD ceiling, noting that even the low estimate (~3.5% of total budget) requires Director General of IT Infrastructure approval, and the PM should assess whether cumulative variances risk breaching the 15% Treasury Board threshold; (3) schedule impact of 9 additional days against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint for D-006 — with Go-Live only ~54 days away from the email date, this risk must be formally assessed; (4) written approval from the Project Sponsor prior to any vendor engagement or procurement action. The steering committee discussion Dave references is appropriate as an escalation venue, but only after a formal Change Request is drafted, not as a substitute for it. Additionally, note that Sites B and C are slated for decommissioning — investing in Wi-Fi infrastructure at those sites requires explicit justification and may be inconsistent with the decommissioning mandate.
parsed_json.reasoning: The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clear scope expansion. The approved scope baseline explicitly limits network hardware work to Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switch deployment and associated network segmentation and BGP migration activities. Wireless access point replacement is a distinct hardware category not referenced anywhere in the in-scope items. The out-of-scope exclusion of network monitoring tool upgrades — deferred to a separate initiative — establishes a pattern that infrastructure upgrades beyond the defined baseline are intentionally excluded, and the same logic applies here. The cost estimate of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on an already fully committed $2,400,000 CAD envelope, triggering mandatory Director General approval. The 9-day timeline addition is a material schedule risk given the hard April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006) and the ADM-level approval required for any extension beyond June 30, 2026. Furthermore, Dave's informal engagement with TechSupplier prior to any formal change approval is a procurement governance concern that the PM should address directly. The efficiency argument (contractors already on-site, reduced future disruption) has operational merit and may justify pursuing the change — but only through proper channels, not as an informal add-on.
